reasoning_model_1: The model successfully integrates the required paralinguistic feature. The audio begins with distinct non-linguistic vocalizations (coughing/throat clearing) at 0:00-0:02, effectively simulating the aftermath of choking. The spoken content 'I just choked, it is a bit uncomfortable' follows the script accurately (expanding 'it's' to 'it is' is a negligible deviation). The tone of the speech reflects the physical discomfort mentioned in the text, with appropriate pauses after the coughing fit. The transition from the vocalization to speech is natural.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails completely on content accuracy. The user requested to read a specific English sentence: 'I just choked, it's a bit uncomfortable.' The model instead generated speech in Chinese ('咳嗽, 我刚才呛到了, 有点不舒服'). Furthermore, it appears to have read the action 'Coughing' as the word '咳嗽' (Kesou) or simply translated the prompt, rather than performing the non-linguistic vocalization naturally alongside the English text. Due to the wrong language and failure to adhere to the script, this is a score of 0.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed the instructions perfectly, generating the correct English text and integrating realistic coughing sounds (non-linguistic vocalizations) as requested. The execution was natural and consistent with the scenario of choking. Model 2 failed to generate the correct language, speaking in Chinese instead of English, and did not effectively demonstrate the requested non-linguistic feature in the context of the requested script. Therefore, Model 1 is the clear winner.
